We'll need to read those files before modifying. We can iterate with commands but we need to do per-file actions one at a time.

We need backup before editing: cp <file> <file>.bak

We need to apply runtime: sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding=0 (but this command is prohibited because it's in prior attempts; we must not repeat that exact command). Maybe use echo "net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0" > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/forwarding? Actually sysctl -w is simplest but prohibited? The list of avoided includes "sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding=0". So can't use that command. Alternative: write directly to /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/forwarding: echo 0 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/forwarding . That would set runtime without sysctl.

Also need to reload sysctl: sysctl --system is prohibited; we could run sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/10-ipv4-forwarding.conf but that only loads that file; allowed? Not listed as prohibited. So we can use sysctl -p <file>.

Thus plan:

1. Identify all files that may contain net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding (non-commented). We'll examine each of /etc/sysctl.conf, /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf, /run/sysctl.d/*.conf, /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf.

2. For each file, read it. If contains non-commented line matching net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding, then backup (cp file file.bak) and comment out the line via sed -i 's/^\(\s*\)net\.ipv4\.conf\.all\.forwarding\s*=\s*/\1# &/' file . Must ensure not to duplicate.

3. Create a new persistent file: /etc/sysctl.d/10-ipv4-forwarding.conf with line "net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0". Ensure file does not exist; if exists, backup.

4. Apply runtime: echo 0 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/forwarding.

5. Reload sysctl for that file: sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/10-ipv4-forwarding.conf .
